mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payroll employment signals expanding aggregate demand and wage income, which increases expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5 percent, the zero lower bound constraint on monetary policy eliminates short-term interest income, inducing portfolio rebalancing into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal a broadening of aggregate demand recovery, reducing left-tail recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of concurrent months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals that labor market slack is being absorbed faster than discounted, lifting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8%, a one-month decline in UNRATE is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales signal robust consumer spending, which is the largest component of GDP, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising consumer price index combined with a federal funds rate below one percent signals that monetary policy is behind the curve, forcing an eventual aggressive tightening shock that is not yet priced into equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: Months where CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS is below 1% are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter acceleration in real GDP growth signals a strengthening economic expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with annualized GDPC1 growth above 2.5% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and falling capacity utilization, which compresses cyclical profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.
